title: Augmented Human Development Index
subtitle: The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.
caption:
Augmented Human Development Index

The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.

This choropleth world map displays the Augmented Human Development Index for the year 2020. The legend at the bottom defines the color scale, ranging from 0 to 0.8, with a separate hatched pattern indicating "No data." The color gradient transitions from very pale blue for the lowest values to deep navy blue for the highest values. The darkest shades, representing index values approaching or exceeding 0.8, are visible in Australia, New Zealand, Canada, the United States, and a cluster of nations in Western and Northern Europe, including Norway, Sweden, and Germany. Japan and South Korea also appear in these darker blue bands. In contrast, the lightest shades of blue, corresponding to values near the bottom of the scale between 0 and 0.2, cover a broad area of Sub-Saharan Africa, including countries such as Niger, Chad, and the Central African Republic. Much of South Asia and parts of Southeast Asia sit in the lower-middle range of the legend, appearing in pale to medium blue tones. Latin America displays a mix of shades, with countries like Argentina and Chile appearing in darker blues than their northern neighbors. Russia and China are depicted in mid-range blue tones. Greenland and a few other small territories show the hatched "No data" pattern. The visual distribution places the highest index scores in North America, Western Europe, and Oceania, while the lowest scores are concentrated in Central and Sub-Saharan Africa.
